research_question: Which accountability facts should be portable, referenced, cryptographically committed or excluded when audit evidence crosses organisational boundaries in health information exchange?
unit_of_analysis: candidate fact in one declared healthcare event
required_input: frozen accountability questions; synthetic source context; Consent and policy versions; actor and organisation context; resource and provenance context
processing: classify each candidate fact; test necessity through case reconstruction and field deletion; test exposure through payload scans
evidence_output: field-classification matrix; accountability coverage; field-deletion results; privacy findings
falsification_condition: The declared accountability questions cannot be answered without carrying raw clinical payload or an unbounded direct identifier.

research_question: How can the boundary be operationalised through a signed TrustEvidence envelope and a FHIR R4/BALP-facing projection without copying the underlying clinical payload?
unit_of_analysis: one signed envelope and its structured FHIR projection
required_input: RQ1 field decisions; closed envelope schema; exact source-resource versions; commitment inputs; FHIR R4 definitions; applicable BALP patterns
processing: construct and validate the envelope; canonicalise and sign exact bytes; project selected semantics to FHIR; preserve the envelope bytes; execute round-trip checks
evidence_output: signed envelope; FHIR evidence bundle; official validator outcomes; round-trip and byte-preservation reports
falsification_condition: A mandatory semantic field is lost or altered by the FHIR projection; positive examples fail mandatory validation; the portable bundle contains forbidden clinical content.

research_question: Which structural, semantic, signature, commitment, receipt and retained-checkpoint properties are established by the executed validation and mutation corpus?
unit_of_analysis: one candidate envelope or local A2 receipt under an explicit verification policy
required_input: positive corpus; negative corpus; mutation registry; issuer and backend public keys; prior checkpoint state where required
processing: run schema and semantic validation; verify canonical bytes and signatures; verify commitments; verify local receipt binding, inclusion and consistency; reject controlled mutations
evidence_output: property-specific verification report; false-accept count; expected-rejection matrix; residual-risk statement
falsification_condition: Any critical mutation is accepted; invalid verification advances state; a reported property depends on an unexecuted external mechanism.

research_question: What local incremental processing and message-size cost does the complete reference pipeline introduce relative to the declared source-processing and conventional audit-projection baselines?
unit_of_analysis: one independent paired run over the same frozen workload sequence
required_input: frozen disclosure and wearable-batch workloads; B0-B2 implementations; preregistered seeds; fixed environment; retained raw timings
processing: execute minimum twenty paired independent runs after a separate pilot and warm-up; calculate run-level p50 p95 and p99; calculate paired time and byte increments
evidence_output: raw run files; run-level summaries; paired increments B1-B0 B2-B1 and B2-B0; message-size and failure summaries
falsification_condition: Baselines do not process identical source inputs; independent-run replication is absent; failures or slow tails are removed; results cannot be regenerated.
